title: matelea atrostellata
intro: Matelea atrostellata, also known as the "Coral Milkflower," is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been utilized for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Matelea atrostellata include its potential to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Herbal preparations of Matelea atrostellata can be made by infusing the plant's leaves in hot water to create a tea or by using its extracts in tinctures and ointments.
